content:
• While resolving card abilities, if 'you' can be resolved as referring to the player's identity, it must be so resolved (damage to 'you' applies to the identity's hit point dial; indirect damage to 'you' can be assigned to controlled characters; exhausting/status-carding 'you' applies to the identity; game functions performed 'by you' must be performed by the controlled identity if able — exception: 'after [enemy] attacks you' refers to the attacked player even if they defended with an ally).
  • If 'you' cannot be resolved as referring to the identity, it refers to the player (discarding from 'your' hand, searching 'your' deck, performing game functions against non-identity cards 'you' control, or triggering from a game function 'you' perform that cannot refer to the identity).
  • Cards considered an extension of a player's identity: Events, Resources, Upgrades (unless attached to a different friendly character).
  • Cards NOT considered an extension: Allies, Encounter cards, Player Side Schemes, Supports.
